565	def get_pythonized_sample_results(
566	        sample_result_args: SampleResultArgsType) -> SampleResultType:
567	    '''This function consumes GPU-side sampler results and computes
568	    Pythonized CPU-side sampler results (GPU -> CPU sync.)
569	
570	    Single-step scheduling: this function is invoked at sampling-time
571	    for immediate Pythonization.
572	
573	    Multi-step scheduling: Pythonization is deferred until after multiple
574	    GPU-side steps have been completed.
575	
576	    Args:
577	      sample_result_args: GPU-side inputs to the Pythonization process
578	
579	    Returns:
580	      Pythonized sampler results
581	    '''
582	
583	    (
584	        sample_metadata,
585	        sampling_metadata,
586	        greedy_samples,
587	        multinomial_samples,
588	        sample_results_dict,
589	    ) = (
590	        sample_result_args.sample_metadata,
591	        sample_result_args.sampling_metadata,
592	        sample_result_args.greedy_samples,
593	        sample_result_args.multinomial_samples,
594	        sample_result_args.sample_results_dict,
595	    )
596	
597	    for sampling_type in SamplingType:
598	        if sampling_type not in sample_metadata:
599	            continue
600	        (seq_group_id, seq_groups) = sample_metadata[sampling_type]
601	        if sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
602	            sample_results = _greedy_sample(seq_groups, greedy_samples)
603	        elif sampling_type in (SamplingType.RANDOM, SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED):
604	            sample_results = _random_sample(seq_groups,
605	                                            multinomial_samples[sampling_type])
606	        sample_results_dict.update(zip(seq_group_id, sample_results))
607	
608	    return [
609	        sample_results_dict.get(i, ([], []))
610	        for i in range(len(sampling_metadata.seq_groups))
611	    ]
612

169	class Sampler(nn.Module):
170	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
171	
172	    This layer does the following:
173	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
174	        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
175	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
176	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
177	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
178	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
179	    6. Sample the next tokens.
180	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
181	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
182	
183	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
184	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
185	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
186	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
187	    in logits for each token in the input prompt.
188	    """
189	
190	    def __init__(self):
191	        super().__init__()
192	
193	        # Whether or not the SamplerOutput should have on-device tensors
194	        # containing the sampled token ids and probabilities. This is used by
195	        # speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
196	        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
197	        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False
198	
199	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
200	        self,
201	        logits: torch.Tensor,
202	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
203	    ):
204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None
214	
215	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
216	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
217	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
218	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
219	
220	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
221	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
222	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
223	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
224	
225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """

111	    sampled_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
112	    # CPU tensor containing the sampled token ids. Used during multi-step to
113	    # return the sampled token ids from last rank to AsyncLLMEngine to be
114	    # 'broadcasted' to all other PP ranks for next step.
115	    sampled_token_ids_cpu: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
116	
117	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token embeddings (embeddings
118	    # corresponding to the sampled token ids). Used when prompt embeddings are
119	    # specified in lieu of prompt token ids or text.
120	    sampled_token_embeds: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
121	
122	    # Spec decode metrics populated by workers.
123	    spec_decode_worker_metrics: Optional[SpecDecodeWorkerMetrics] = None
124	
125	    # Optional last hidden states from the model.
126	    hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
127	
128	    # Optional prefill hidden states from the model
129	    # (used for models like EAGLE).
130	    prefill_hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
131	
132	    # Time taken in the forward pass for this across all workers
133	    model_forward_time: Optional[float] = None
134	
135	    # Time taken in the model execute function. This will include model forward,
136	    # block/sync across workers, cpu-gpu sync time and sampling time.
137	    model_execute_time: Optional[float] = None
138	
139	    def __getitem__(self, idx: int) -> CompletionSequenceGroupOutput:
140	        return self.outputs[idx]
141	
142	    def __setitem__(self, idx: int, value):
143	        self.outputs[idx] = value
144	
145	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]:
146	        return iter(self.outputs)
147	
148	    def __len__(self):
149	        return len(self.outputs)
150	
151	    def __eq__(self, other: object):
152	        return isinstance(other,
153	                          self.__class__) and self.outputs == other.outputs
154	
155	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
156	        """Show the shape of a tensor instead of its values to reduce noise.
157	        """
158	        sampled_token_probs_repr = ("None" if self.sampled_token_probs is None
159	                                    else self.sampled_token_probs.shape)
160	        sampled_token_ids_repr = ("None" if self.sampled_token_ids is None else
161	                                  self.sampled_token_ids.shape)
162	        return (
163	            f"SamplerOutput(outputs={self.outputs}, "
164	            f"sampled_token_probs={sampled_token_probs_repr}, "
165	            f"sampled_token_ids={sampled_token_ids_repr}, "
166	            f"spec_decode_worker_metrics={self.spec_decode_worker_metrics})")
167

169	class Sampler(nn.Module):
170	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
171	
172	    This layer does the following:
173	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
174	        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
175	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
176	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
177	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
178	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
179	    6. Sample the next tokens.
180	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
181	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
182	
183	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
184	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
185	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
186	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
187	    in logits for each token in the input prompt.
188	    """
189	
190	    def __init__(self):
191	        super().__init__()
192	
193	        # Whether or not the SamplerOutput should have on-device tensors
194	        # containing the sampled token ids and probabilities. This is used by
195	        # speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
196	        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
197	        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False
198	
199	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
200	        self,
201	        logits: torch.Tensor,
202	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
203	    ):
204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None
214	
215	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
216	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
217	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
218	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
219	
220	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
221	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
222	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
223	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
224	
225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """
250	        assert logits is not None
251	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
252	
253	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
254	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
255	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
256	        elif self._do_penalties:
257	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
258	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
259	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
260	            # between decode runs.
261	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
262	
263	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
264	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
265	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
266	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
267	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
268	
269	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
270	
271	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
272	        if do_penalties:
273	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
274	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
275	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
277	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
278	
279	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
280	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
281	        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
282	        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
283	
284	        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
285	            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
286	                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)
287	
288	        if do_min_p:
289	            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
290	
291	        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
292	        # Compute the probabilities.
293	        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
294	        # Compute the log probabilities.
295	        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
296	
297	        # Sample the next tokens.
298	        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
299	            probs,
300	            logprobs,

4	from array import array
5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	from typing import Optional

8	import torch

10	from vllm.sampling_params import SamplingParams, SamplingType
11	from vllm.sequence import (VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE, SequenceData,
12	                           SequenceGroupMetadata)
13	from vllm.utils import (PyObjectCache, async_tensor_h2d,
14	                        is_pin_memory_available, make_tensor_with_pad)

16	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5

19	@dataclass
20	class SequenceGroupToSample:
21	    # |---------- N-1 iteration --------|
22	    # |---------------- N iteration ---------------------|
23	    # |- tokenA -|......................|-- newTokens ---|
24	    # |---------- context_len ----------|
25	    # |-------------------- seq_len ----------------------|
26	    #                                   |-- query_len ---|
27	
28	    # Sequence ids for the sequence group in a previous step.
29	    seq_ids: list[int]
30	    sampling_params: SamplingParams
31	    # seq_id -> sequence data.
32	    seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]
33	    # The length of the sequence (all tokens seen in the past + new token to
34	    # compute attention) of the sequence group. None if it is in a decode
35	    # stage.
36	    seq_len: Optional[int]
37	    # The length of new query tokens to compute in the current step. None if it
38	    # is in a decode stage. The length of query_len <= seq_len if chunked
39	    # prefill is enabled.
40	    query_len: Optional[int]
41	    # A random number generator for sampling.
42	    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
43	    # True if the sequence group is in prefill stage. False if it is in a
44	    # decode stage.
45	    is_prompt: bool
46	    # Query token indices from logits. to compute prompt logprob. Empty if
47	    # prompt logprob is not required.
48	    prompt_logprob_indices: list[int]
49	    # Sample token indices from logits. Empty if sampling is not required.
50	    sample_indices: list[int]
51	
52	    @property
53	    def do_sample(self):
54	        return len(self.sample_indices) > 0
55	
56	    def __post_init__(self):
57	        if len(self.prompt_logprob_indices) > 0:
58	            assert self.sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None
59	        if self.is_prompt:
60	            assert self.seq_len is not None
61	            assert self.query_len is not None
62	
63	
64	def gen_seq_group_to_sample_builder(num_seqs: int):
65	    return lambda: SequenceGroupToSample(
66	        seq_ids=[0] * num_seqs,
67	        sampling_params=None,
68	        seq_data=None,  # type: ignore
69	        seq_len=0,
70	        query_len=0,
71	        generator=None,
72	        is_prompt=True,
73	        prompt_logprob_indices=[],
74	        sample_indices=[],
75	    )
76

78	class SamplingMetadataCache:
79	    """Used to cache SamplingMetadata objects between scheduler iterations"""
80	
81	    def __init__(self):
82	        self._seq_group_to_sample_cache: dict[int, PyObjectCache] = {}
83	
84	    def get_cached_seq_group_to_sample(self, num_seqs):
85	        if num_seqs not in self._seq_group_to_sample_cache:
86	            self._seq_group_to_sample_cache[num_seqs] = PyObjectCache(
87	                gen_seq_group_to_sample_builder(num_seqs))
88	
89	        obj = self._seq_group_to_sample_cache[num_seqs].get_object()
90	        return obj
91	
92	    def reset(self):
93	        for cache in self._seq_group_to_sample_cache.values():
94	            cache.reset()

97	class SamplingMetadata:
98	    """Metadata for input sequences. Used in sampler.
99	
100	    The usage is as follow;
101	    ```
102	    hidden_states = execute_model(...)
103	    logits = hidden_states[sampling_metadata.selected_token_indices]
104	    sample(logits)
105	
106	    def sample(logits):
107	        # Use categorized_sample_indices for sampling....
108	    ```
109	
110	    Args:
111	        seq_groups: List of batched sequence groups.
112	        selected_token_indices: (num_query_tokens_to_logprob). Indices to find
113	            logits from the initial model output hidden states.
114	        categorized_sample_indices: SamplingType -> token indices to sample.
115	            Each token indices is 2D tensor of (num_indices, num_indices) where
116	            the first item means the sample index within the returned logit
117	            (before pruning padding), and the second item means the sample
118	            index after pruning using selected_token_indices.
119	            For example, if the returned logit is [1, 2, 3], and we select
120	            [1, 2] for sampling, the pruned logit will be [2, 3]. In this case,
121	            The first tuple is [1, 2] (sampled index within original logit),
122	            and the second tuple is [0, 1] (sampled index within pruned logit).
123	        num_prompts: Number of prompt sequence groups in seq_groups.
124	        skip_sampler_cpu_output: Indicates if we want to skip the GPU=>CPU
125	            serialization of token outputs.
126	        reuse_sampling_tensors: Indicates if we want to reuse sampling
127	            tensors that are part of the sampler forward pass. Currently,
128	            it is mainly used for multi-step decode.
129	
130	    """
131	
132	    def __init__(
133	        self,
134	        seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
135	        selected_token_indices: torch.Tensor,
136	        categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor],
137	        num_prompts: int,
138	        skip_sampler_cpu_output: bool = False,
139	        reuse_sampling_tensors: bool = False,
140	    ) -> None:
141	        self.seq_groups = seq_groups
142	        self.selected_token_indices = selected_token_indices
143	        self.categorized_sample_indices = categorized_sample_indices
144	        self.num_prompts = num_prompts
145	        self.skip_sampler_cpu_output = skip_sampler_cpu_output
146	        self.reuse_sampling_tensors = reuse_sampling_tensors
147	
148	    @staticmethod
149	    def prepare(
150	        seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],
151	        seq_lens: list[int],
152	        query_lens: list[int],
153	        device: str,
154	        pin_memory: bool,
155	        generators: Optional[dict[str, torch.Generator]] = None,
156	        cache: Optional[SamplingMetadataCache] = None,
157	    ) -> "SamplingMetadata":
158	        (
159	            seq_groups,
160	            selected_token_indices,
161	            categorized_sample_indices,
162	            num_prompts,
163	        ) = _prepare_seq_groups(seq_group_metadata_list, seq_lens, query_lens,
164	                                device, generators, cache)
165	        selected_token_indices = async_tensor_h2d(
166	            selected_token_indices,
167	            dtype=torch.long,
168	            target_device=device,
169	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
170	        )
171	        categorized_sample_indices = {
172	            t:
173	            async_tensor_h2d(
174	                seq_ids,
175	                dtype=torch.int,
176	                target_device=device,
177	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
178	            )
179	            for t, seq_ids in categorized_sample_indices.items()
180	        }
181	
182	        sampling_metadata = SamplingMetadata(
183	            seq_groups=seq_groups,
184	            selected_token_indices=selected_token_indices,
185	            categorized_sample_indices=categorized_sample_indices,
186	            num_prompts=num_prompts,
187	        )
188	        return sampling_metadata
189	
190	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
191	        return (
192	            "SamplingMetadata("
193	            f"seq_groups={self.seq_groups}, "
194	            f"selected_token_indices={self.selected_token_indices}, "
195	            f"categorized_sample_indices={self.categorized_sample_indices})")
196	
197	
198	def _prepare_seq_groups(
199	    seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],
200	    seq_lens: list[int],
201	    query_lens: list[int],
202	    device: str,
203	    generators: Optional[dict[str, torch.Generator]] = None,
204	    cache: Optional[SamplingMetadataCache] = None,
205	) -> tuple[
206	        list[SequenceGroupToSample],
207	        list[int],
208	        dict[SamplingType, list[int]],
209	        int,
210	]:
211	    """Prepare sequence groups and indices for sampling.
212	
213	    Args:
214	        seq_group_metadata_list: A list of sequence group to batch.
215	        seq_lens: A list of sequence lens per sequence group.
216	            Index of prompt len should match with seq_group_metadata_list.
217	        query_lens: A list of query lengths. Prompt lens include the length
218	            of entire prompt tokens, and it could be shorter.
219	        device: A device to use for random number generators,
220	            `SequenceGroupToSample.generator`.
221	        generators: A store of per-request random number generators used
222	            for seeded requests.
223	
224	    Returns:
225	        seq_groups: A list of sequence group to sample.
226	        selected_token_indices: See the definition from `SamplingMetadata`.
227	        categorized_sample_indices: See the definition from `SamplingMetadata`.
228	        num_prompts: Total number of prompts from `seq_group_metadata_list`.
229	    """
230	    # Batched sequence groups for the current model forward stsep.
231	    seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample] = []
232	    # A list of token indices to sample/compute logprob. It is used to
233	    # prune the outcome logits from the model for the performance.
234	    selected_token_indices: list[int] = []
235	    # Used for selected_token_indices.
236	    model_output_idx = 0
237	
238	    # Sampling type -> (
239	    # indices to sample/prompt logprob within pruned output logits,
240	    # indices to sample within pruned logits)
241	    categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, list[int]] = {
242	        t: []
243	        for t in SamplingType
244	    }
245	    # Index of logits to compute logprob. Logits include both prompt logprob
246	    # and sample logprob indices.
247	    logit_idx = 0
248	    # Total number of prompts from given sequence groups.
249	    num_prompts = 0
250	
251	    for i, seq_group_metadata in enumerate(seq_group_metadata_list):
252	        seq_ids = seq_group_metadata.seq_data.keys()
253	
254	        if cache is not None:
255	            sample_obj = cache.get_cached_seq_group_to_sample(len(seq_ids))
256	
257	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
258	                sample_obj.seq_ids[j] = seq_id
259	
260	            sample_obj.prompt_logprob_indices.clear()
261	            sample_obj.sample_indices.clear()
262	
263	        sampling_params = seq_group_metadata.sampling_params
264	        is_prompt = seq_group_metadata.is_prompt
265	        generator: Optional[torch.Generator] = None
266	        # If the current seq group is in decode stage, it is None.
267	        seq_len: Optional[int] = None
268	        query_len: Optional[int] = None
269	        prompt_logprob_indices: list[int] = (sample_obj.prompt_logprob_indices
270	                                             if cache is not None else [])
271	        sample_indices: list[int] = (sample_obj.sample_indices
272	                                     if cache is not None else [])
273	        do_sample = seq_group_metadata.do_sample
274	
275	        if seq_group_metadata.is_prompt:
276	            if sampling_params.seed is not None:
277	                generator = torch.Generator(device=device).manual_seed(
278	                    sampling_params.seed)
279	                if generators is not None:
280	                    generators[seq_group_metadata.request_id] = generator
281	
282	            num_prompts += 1
283	            num_prefill_sample = len(seq_ids)
284	            assert num_prefill_sample == 1
285	            assert query_lens is not None and seq_lens is not None
286	            query_len, seq_len = query_lens[i], seq_lens[i]
287	            # If we need sampling, exclude num_prefill_sample tokens from
288	            # prompt logprob.
289	            prompt_logprob_len = (query_len - num_prefill_sample
290	                                  if do_sample else query_len)
291	            sample_len = num_prefill_sample if do_sample else 0
292	        else:
293	            # Decode
294	            prompt_logprob_len = 0
295	            query_len = query_lens[i] if query_lens is not None and len(
296	                query_lens) > 0 else 1
297	            sample_len = len(seq_ids) * query_len if do_sample else 0
298	
299	            if sampling_params.seed is not None and generators is not None:
300	                generator = generators.get(seq_group_metadata.request_id)
301	
302	        # Update indices to select from the model output.
303	        """
304	        This blocks computes selected_token_indices which is used in the
305	        following way.
306	
307	        hidden_states = model(...)
308	        logits = hidden_states[selected_token_indices]
309	        """
310	
311	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
312	            selected_token_indices.extend(
313	                range(model_output_idx, model_output_idx + prompt_logprob_len))
314	        model_output_idx += prompt_logprob_len
315	        if do_sample:
316	            selected_token_indices.extend(
317	                range(model_output_idx, model_output_idx + sample_len))
318	        model_output_idx += sample_len
319	
320	        # We now find indices for logprob computation and sampling.
321	        """
322	        This block computes categorized_sample_indices which is used in the
323	        following way.
324	
325	        hidden_states = model(...)
326	        logits = hidden_states[selected_token_indices]
327	        def sample(logits):
328	           # Use categorized_sample_indices for sampling.
329	           # prompt_logprob_indices to find prompt logprob indices.
330	           # sample_indices to find sample indices.
331	        """
332	
333	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
334	            prompt_logprob_indices.extend(
335	                range(logit_idx, logit_idx + prompt_logprob_len))
336	            logit_idx += prompt_logprob_len
337	        if do_sample:
338	            sample_indices.extend(range(logit_idx, logit_idx + sample_len))
339	            categorized_sample_indices[sampling_params.sampling_type].extend(
340	                list(range(logit_idx, logit_idx + sample_len)))
341	            logit_idx += sample_len
342	
343	        if cache is not None:
344	            sample_obj.sampling_params = sampling_params
345	            sample_obj.seq_data = seq_group_metadata.seq_data
346	            sample_obj.seq_len = seq_len
347	            sample_obj.query_len = query_len
348	            sample_obj.generator = generator
349	            sample_obj.is_prompt = is_prompt
350	        else:
351	            sample_obj = SequenceGroupToSample(
352	                seq_ids=list(seq_ids),
353	                sampling_params=sampling_params,
354	                seq_data=seq_group_metadata.seq_data,
355	                seq_len=seq_len,
356	                query_len=query_len,
357	                generator=generator,
358	                is_prompt=is_prompt,
359	                prompt_logprob_indices=list(prompt_logprob_indices),
360	                sample_indices=list(sample_indices),
361	            )
362	
363	        seq_groups.append(sample_obj)
364	
365	    if cache is not None:
366	        cache.reset()
367	
368	    return (seq_groups, selected_token_indices, categorized_sample_indices,
369	            num_prompts)

372	@dataclass
373	class SamplingTensors:
374	    """Tensors for sampling."""
375	
376	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
377	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
378	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
379	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
380	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
381	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
382	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
383	    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
384	    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
385	
386	    @classmethod
387	    def from_sampling_metadata(
388	        cls,
389	        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
390	        vocab_size: int,
391	        device: torch.device,
392	        dtype: torch.dtype,
393	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
394	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
395	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        top_ks: list[int] = []
397	        temperatures: list[float] = []
398	        top_ps: list[float] = []
399	        min_ps: list[float] = []
400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
401	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False
406	
407	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
408	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
409	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
410	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
411	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
412	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
413	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
414	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
415	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
416	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
417	
418	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
419	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
420	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
421	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
422	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
423	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
424	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
425	                temperature = 1.0
426	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
427	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
428	                do_top_p_top_k = True
429	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
430	                do_min_p = True
431	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
432	                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
433	                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
434	                do_penalties = True
435	
436	            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
437	            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
438	                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
439	                # their logprobs
440	                query_len = seq_group.query_len
441	                assert query_len is not None
442	                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
443	                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
444	                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
445	                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
446	                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
447	                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
448	                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
449	                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len
450	
451	            if seq_group.do_sample:
452	                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
453	                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
454	                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
455	                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
456	                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
457	                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
458	                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
459	                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
460	                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens
461	
462	        if do_penalties:
463	            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
464	                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
465	                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
466	                if (seq_group.is_prompt
467	                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
468	                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
469	                    prompt_tokens.extend(
470	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
471	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
472	                    output_tokens.extend(
473	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
474	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
475	                if seq_group.do_sample:
476	                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
477	                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
478	                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
479	                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)
480	
481	        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
482	            temperatures,
483	            top_ps,
484	            top_ks,
485	            min_ps,
486	            presence_penalties,
487	            frequency_penalties,
488	            repetition_penalties,
489	            prompt_tokens,
490	            output_tokens,
491	            vocab_size,
492	            device,
493	            dtype,
494	        )
495	        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)
496	
497	    @classmethod
498	    def from_lists(
499	        cls,
500	        temperatures: list[float],
501	        top_ps: list[float],
502	        top_ks: list[int],
503	        min_ps: list[float],
504	        presence_penalties: list[float],
505	        frequency_penalties: list[float],
506	        repetition_penalties: list[float],
507	        prompt_tokens: list[array],
508	        output_tokens: list[array],
509	        vocab_size: int,
510	        device: torch.device,
511	        dtype: torch.dtype,
512	    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
513	        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
514	        # pinned memory.
515	        pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
516	
517	        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens
518	
519	        if do_penalties:
520	            prompt_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
521	                prompt_tokens,
522	                vocab_size,
523	                device="cpu",
524	                dtype=torch.int64,
525	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
526	            )
527	            output_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
528	                output_tokens,
529	                vocab_size,
530	                device="cpu",
531	                dtype=torch.int64,
532	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
533	            )
534	        else:
535	            empty_tensor = torch.empty(0, device=device, dtype=torch.long)
536	            prompt_t = empty_tensor
537	            output_t = empty_tensor
538	
539	        temperatures_t = torch.tensor(
540	            temperatures,
541	            device="cpu",
542	            dtype=dtype,
543	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
544	        )
545	        top_ps_t = torch.tensor(
546	            top_ps,
547	            device="cpu",
548	            dtype=dtype,
549	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
550	        )
551	        min_ps_t = torch.tensor(
552	            min_ps,
553	            device="cpu",
554	            dtype=dtype,
555	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
556	        )
557	        presence_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
558	            presence_penalties,
559	            device="cpu",
560	            dtype=dtype,
561	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
562	        )
563	        frequency_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
564	            frequency_penalties,
565	            device="cpu",
566	            dtype=dtype,
567	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
568	        )
569	        repetition_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
570	            repetition_penalties,
571	            device="cpu",
572	            dtype=dtype,
573	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
574	        )
575	        top_ks_t = torch.tensor(
576	            top_ks,
577	            device="cpu",
578	            dtype=torch.int,
579	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
580	        )
581	        # Because the memory is pinned, we can do non-blocking
582	        # transfer to device.
583	
584	        return cls(
585	            temperatures=temperatures_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
586	            top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
587	            top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
588	            min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
589	            presence_penalties=presence_penalties_t.to(device=device,
590	                                                       non_blocking=True),
591	            frequency_penalties=frequency_penalties_t.to(device=device,
592	                                                         non_blocking=True),
593	            repetition_penalties=repetition_penalties_t.to(device=device,
594	                                                           non_blocking=True),
595	            prompt_tokens=prompt_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
596	            output_tokens=output_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
597	        )
598

27	@dataclass
28	class CachedRequestState:
29	
30	    req_id: str
31	    prompt_token_ids: list[int]
32	    mm_inputs: list[MultiModalKwargs]
33	    mm_positions: list[PlaceholderRange]
34	    sampling_params: Optional[SamplingParams]
35	    pooling_params: Optional[PoolingParams]
36	    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
37	
38	    block_ids: tuple[list[int], ...]
39	    num_computed_tokens: int
40	    output_token_ids: list[int]
41	
42	    mrope_positions: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
43	    mrope_position_delta: Optional[int] = None
44	
45	    lora_request: Optional[LoRARequest] = None
46	
47	    def __post_init__(self):
48	        self.num_prompt_tokens = len(self.prompt_token_ids)
49	
50	    @property
51	    def num_tokens(self) -> int:
52	        return self.num_prompt_tokens + len(self.output_token_ids)
53	
54	    def get_token_id(self, idx: int) -> int:
55	        if idx < self.num_prompt_tokens:
56	            return self.prompt_token_ids[idx]
57	        else:
58	            return self.output_token_ids[idx - self.num_prompt_tokens]

63	    def __init__(
64	        self,
65	        max_num_reqs: int,

71	        block_sizes: list[int],  # The block_size of each kv cache group
72	        is_spec_decode: bool = False,
73	        logits_processing_needs_token_ids: bool = False,
74	    ):
75	        self.is_spec_decode = is_spec_decode
76	        self.max_num_reqs = max_num_reqs

79	        self.device = device

81	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size

84	
85	        self._req_ids: list[Optional[str]] = []
86	        self.req_id_to_index: dict[str, int] = {}
87	
88	        # TODO(woosuk): This buffer could be too large if max_model_len is big.
89	        # Find a way to reduce the CPU memory usage.
90	        # This buffer is not directly transferred to the GPU, so it does not
91	        # need to be pinned.
92	        self.token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
93	            (max_num_reqs, max_model_len),

95	            dtype=torch.int32,
96	            pin_memory=False,
97	        )
98	        self.token_ids_cpu = self.token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
99	        self.num_tokens = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
100	        self.num_tokens_no_spec = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
101	        self.num_prompt_tokens = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
102	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
103	            (max_num_reqs, ),
104	            device="cpu",
105	            dtype=torch.int32,
106	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
107	        )
108	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu = \
109	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor.numpy()
110	
111	        # Block table.
112	        self.block_table = MultiGroupBlockTable(
113	            max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs,
114	            max_model_len=max_model_len,
115	            max_num_batched_tokens=max_num_batched_tokens,
116	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
117	            device=device,
118	            block_sizes=block_sizes,
119	        )
120	
121	        # Sampling-related.
122	        self.temperature = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
123	                                       dtype=torch.float32,
124	                                       device=device)
125	        self.temperature_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
126	                                                  dtype=torch.float32,
127	                                                  device="cpu",
128	                                                  pin_memory=pin_memory)
129	        self.temperature_cpu = self.temperature_cpu_tensor.numpy()
130	        self.greedy_reqs: set[str] = set()
131	        self.random_reqs: set[str] = set()
132	
133	        self.top_p = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
134	                                 dtype=torch.float32,
135	                                 device=device)
136	        self.top_p_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
137	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
138	                                            device="cpu",
139	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
140	        self.top_p_cpu = self.top_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
141	        self.top_p_reqs: set[str] = set()
142	
143	        self.top_k = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
144	                                 dtype=torch.int32,
145	                                 device=device)
146	        self.top_k_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
147	                                            dtype=torch.int32,
148	                                            device="cpu",
149	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
150	        self.top_k_cpu = self.top_k_cpu_tensor.numpy()
151	        self.top_k_reqs: set[str] = set()
152	
153	        # IDs of requests which do not support spec decoding
154	        self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs: set[str] = set()
155	
156	        # Frequency penalty related data structures
157	        self.frequency_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
158	                                               dtype=torch.float,
159	                                               device=device)
160	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
161	            (max_num_reqs, ),
162	            dtype=torch.float,
163	            device="cpu",
164	            pin_memory=pin_memory)
165	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu = \
166	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy()
167	        self.frequency_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
168	
169	        # Presence penalty related data structures
170	        self.presence_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
171	                                              dtype=torch.float,
172	                                              device=device)
173	        self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
174	                                                         dtype=torch.float,
175	                                                         device="cpu",
176	                                                         pin_memory=pin_memory)
177	        self.presence_penalties_cpu = self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy(
178	        )
179	        self.presence_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
180	
181	        # Repetition penalty related data structures
182	        self.repetition_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
183	                                                dtype=torch.float,
184	                                                device=device)
185	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
186	            (max_num_reqs, ),
187	            dtype=torch.float,
188	            device="cpu",
189	            pin_memory=pin_memory)
190	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu = \
191	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy()
192	        self.repetition_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
193	
194	        # lora related
195	        self.request_lora_mapping = np.zeros((self.max_num_reqs, ),
196	                                             dtype=np.int32)
197	        self.lora_id_to_request_ids: dict[int, set[str]] = {}
198	        self.lora_id_to_lora_request: dict[int, LoRARequest] = {}
199	
200	        # req_index -> generator
201	        # NOTE(woosuk): The indices of the requests that do not have their own
202	        # generator should not be included in the dictionary.
203	        self.generators: dict[int, torch.Generator] = {}
204	
205	        self.num_logprobs: dict[str, int] = {}
206	        # NOTE(rob): num_prompt_logprobs only includes reqs
207	        # that are currently in the prefill phase.
208	        self.num_prompt_logprobs: dict[str, int] = {}
209	
210	        # To accumulate prompt logprobs tensor chunks across prefill steps.
211	        self.in_progress_prompt_logprobs_cpu: dict[str, LogprobsTensors] = {}
212	
213	        # Internal representation of per-step batch state changes, used for
214	        # reordering persistent batch and generating logitsprocs batch state
215	        # updates. Should reset each step.
216	        self.batch_update_builder = BatchUpdateBuilder()
217	
218	        # Define logits processors.
219	        # TODO(andy): logits processor list should be extensible via engine
220	        # constructor argument; for now the list is fixed.
221	        self.logitsprocs = init_builtin_logitsprocs(
222	            pin_memory_available=pin_memory,
223	            max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1,
224	            device=device)
225	
226	        # TODO convert this to LogitsProcessor
227	        self.has_allowed_token_ids: set[str] = set()
228	        # NOTE(lufang): In the mask tensor, if the corresponding token allowed,
229	        # the value is False. Since we use masked_fill_ to set -inf.
230	        self.allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
231	        self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
232	
233	        # req_index -> bad_words_token_ids
234	        self.bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]] = {}
235	
236	        self.req_output_token_ids: list[Optional[list[int]]] = []
237	
238	        # This is updated each time the batch constituents change.
239	        self.sampling_metadata = self._make_sampling_metadata()
240	
241	        self.pooling_params: dict[str, PoolingParams] = {}
242	
243	    @property
244	    def req_ids(self) -> list[str]:
245	        # None elements should only be present transiently
246	        # while performing state updates to the batch.
247	        return cast(list[str], self._req_ids)
248	
249	    def _get_next_add_index(self) -> int:
250	        if (req_index := self.batch_update_builder.pop_removed()) is not None:
251	            # Fill the empty index.
252	            return req_index
253	        # Append to end
254	        return self.num_reqs
255	
256	    def _register_add_request(self, request: "CachedRequestState") -> int:
257	        """Track add-request operations"""
258	        req_index = self._get_next_add_index()
259	        assert req_index < self.max_num_reqs
260	        params = (request.sampling_params
261	                  if request.sampling_params else request.pooling_params)
262	        self.batch_update_builder.added.append(
263	            (req_index, params, request.output_token_ids))
264	        return req_index
265	
266	    def add_request(
267	        self,
268	        request: "CachedRequestState",
269	    ) -> int:
270	        req_index = self._register_add_request(request)
271	
272	        req_id = request.req_id
273	        if req_index == len(self._req_ids):
274	            self._req_ids.append(req_id)

225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """
250	        assert logits is not None
251	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
252	
253	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
254	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
255	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
256	        elif self._do_penalties:
257	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
258	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
259	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
260	            # between decode runs.
261	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
262	
263	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
264	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
265	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
266	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
267	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
268	
269	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
270	
271	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
272	        if do_penalties:
273	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
274	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
275	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
277	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
278	
279	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
280	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
281	        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
282	        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
283	
284	        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
285	            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
286	                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)
287	
288	        if do_min_p:
289	            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
290	
291	        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
292	        # Compute the probabilities.
293	        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
294	        # Compute the log probabilities.
295	        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
296	
297	        # Sample the next tokens.
298	        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
299	            probs,
300	            logprobs,
301	            sampling_metadata,
302	            sampling_tensors,
303	            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
304	            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
305	        )
306	
307	        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
308	            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
309	            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
310	            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
311	            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
312	            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
313	        else:
314	            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
315	            # GPU-side tensors.
316	            on_device_tensors = None
317	
318	        # Get the logprobs query results.
319	        prompt_logprobs = None
320	        sample_logprobs = None
321	        if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
322	            # Pythonize logprobs now (GPU -> CPU); do not defer.
323	            assert not isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results,
324	                                  SampleResultArgsType)
325	            prompt_logprobs, sample_logprobs = get_logprobs(
326	                logprobs, sampling_metadata, maybe_deferred_sample_results)
327	
328	        return _build_sampler_output(
329	            maybe_deferred_sample_results,
330	            sampling_metadata,
331	            prompt_logprobs,
332	            sample_logprobs,
333	            on_device_tensors=on_device_tensors,
334	            skip_sampler_cpu_output=sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output)
335	
336	    @property
337	    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
338	        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
339	        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
340	        the greedily-sampled token.
341	
342	        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
343	        sampled token to 1.
344	
345	        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
346	        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
347	        """
348	        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace

600	    def _make_sampling_metadata(self) -> SamplingMetadata:
601	        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
602	        if not self.all_greedy:
603	            temperature = copy_slice(self.temperature_cpu_tensor,
604	                                     self.temperature, num_reqs)
605	        else:
606	            temperature = None
607	        if not self.no_top_p:
608	            copy_slice(self.top_p_cpu_tensor, self.top_p, num_reqs)
609	        if not self.no_top_k:
610	            copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)
611	
612	        if not self.no_penalties:
613	            # Since syncing these tensors is expensive only copy them
614	            # if necessary i.e. if there are requests which require
615	            # penalties to be applied during sampling.
616	            copy_slice(self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor,
617	                       self.frequency_penalties, num_reqs)
618	            copy_slice(self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor,
619	                       self.presence_penalties, num_reqs)
620	            copy_slice(self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor,
621	                       self.repetition_penalties, num_reqs)
622	
623	        needs_prompt_token_ids = (not self.no_penalties or
624	                                  (self.num_reqs > 0
625	                                   and self.logits_processing_needs_token_ids))
626	        if needs_prompt_token_ids:
627	            # The prompt tokens are used only for applying penalties or
628	            # step pooling during the sampling/pooling process.
629	            # Hence copy these tensors only when there are requests which
630	            # need penalties/step_pooler to be applied.
631	            prompt_token_ids = self._make_prompt_token_ids_tensor()
632	        else:
633	            prompt_token_ids = None
634	
635	        allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
636	        if not self.no_allowed_token_ids:
637	            assert self.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None
638	            copy_slice(self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor,
639	                       self.allowed_token_ids_mask, num_reqs)
640	            allowed_token_ids_mask = self.allowed_token_ids_mask[:num_reqs]
641	
642	        return SamplingMetadata(
643	            temperature=temperature,
644	            all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
645	            all_random=self.all_random,
646	            top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
647	            top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
648	            generators=self.generators,
649	            max_num_logprobs=self.max_num_logprobs,
650	            prompt_token_ids=prompt_token_ids,
651	            frequency_penalties=self.frequency_penalties[:num_reqs],
652	            presence_penalties=self.presence_penalties[:num_reqs],
653	            repetition_penalties=self.repetition_penalties[:num_reqs],
654	            output_token_ids=cast(list[list[int]], self.req_output_token_ids),
655	            no_penalties=self.no_penalties,
656	            allowed_token_ids_mask=allowed_token_ids_mask,
657	            bad_words_token_ids=self.bad_words_token_ids,
658	            logitsprocs=self.logitsprocs,
659	        )

661	    @property
662	    def pooling_metadata(self) -> PoolingMetadata:
663	        if len(self.pooling_params) == 0:
664	            pooling_params = []
665	        else:
666	            # Note, for now this assumes that all request in the batch
667	            # are either sampling or pooling requests
668	            assert len(self.req_ids) == len(self.pooling_params)
669	            pooling_params = [
670	                self.pooling_params[req_id] for req_id in self.req_ids
671	            ]
672	
673	        return PoolingMetadata(
674	            prompt_lens=torch.from_numpy(
675	                self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs]).to(self.device),
676	            prompt_token_ids=self.sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
677	            pooling_params=pooling_params,
678	        )

680	    def _make_prompt_token_ids_tensor(self) -> torch.Tensor:
681	        max_prompt_len = self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs].max()
682	        prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
683	            (self.num_reqs, max_prompt_len),
684	            device="cpu",
685	            dtype=torch.int64,
686	            pin_memory=self.pin_memory,
687	        )
688	        prompt_token_ids = prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
689	        prompt_token_ids[:] = self.token_ids_cpu[:self.
690	                                                 num_reqs, :max_prompt_len]
691	        # Use the value of vocab_size as a pad since we don't have a
692	        # token_id of this value.
693	        for i in range(self.num_reqs):
694	            prompt_token_ids[i, self.num_prompt_tokens[i]:] = self.vocab_size
695	        return prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.to(device=self.device,
696	                                              non_blocking=True)

22	    def __init__(
23	            self,
24	            max_num_reqs: int,
25	            max_model_len: int,
26	            max_num_batched_tokens: int,
27	            device: torch.device,
28	            pin_memory: bool,
29	            vocab_size: int,
30	            block_sizes: list[int],  # The block_size of each kv cache group
31	    ):
32	        self.max_num_reqs = max_num_reqs
33	        self.max_model_len = max_model_len
34	        self.max_num_batched_tokens = max_num_batched_tokens
35	        self.device = device
36	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
37	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
38	
39	        self._req_ids: list[Optional[str]] = []
40	        self.req_id_to_index: dict[str, int] = {}
41	
42	        # TODO(woosuk): This buffer could be too large if max_model_len is big.
43	        # Find a way to reduce the CPU memory usage.
44	        # This buffer is not directly transferred to the GPU, so it does not
45	        # need to be pinned.
46	        self.token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
47	            (max_num_reqs, max_model_len),
48	            device="cpu",
49	            dtype=torch.int32,
50	            pin_memory=False,
51	        )
52	        self.token_ids_cpu = self.token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
53	        self.num_tokens = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
54	        self.num_tokens_no_spec = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
55	        self.num_prompt_tokens = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
56	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
57	            (max_num_reqs, ),
58	            device="cpu",
59	            dtype=torch.int32,
60	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
61	        )
62	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu = \
63	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor.numpy()
64	
65	        # Block table.
66	        self.block_table = MultiGroupBlockTable(
67	            max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs,
68	            max_model_len=max_model_len,
69	            max_num_batched_tokens=max_num_batched_tokens,
70	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
71	            device=device,
72	            block_sizes=block_sizes,
73	        )
74	
75	        # Sampling-related.
76	        self.temperature = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
77	                                       dtype=torch.float32,
78	                                       device=device)
79	        self.temperature_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
80	                                                  dtype=torch.float32,
81	                                                  device="cpu",
82	                                                  pin_memory=pin_memory)
83	        self.temperature_cpu = self.temperature_cpu_tensor.numpy()
84	        self.greedy_reqs: set[str] = set()
85	        self.random_reqs: set[str] = set()
86	
87	        self.top_p = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),

196	    def add_request(
197	        self,
198	        request: "CachedRequestState",
199	        req_index: Optional[int] = None,
200	    ) -> None:
201	        if req_index is None:
202	            req_index = self.num_reqs
203	        assert req_index < self.max_num_reqs
204	
205	        req_id = request.req_id
206	        if req_index == len(self._req_ids):
207	            self._req_ids.append(req_id)
208	            self.req_output_token_ids.append(request.output_token_ids)
209	        else:
210	            self._req_ids[req_index] = req_id
211	            self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = request.output_token_ids
212	
213	        self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = req_index
214	
215	        # Copy the prompt token ids and output token ids.
216	        num_prompt_tokens = len(request.prompt_token_ids)
217	        self.num_prompt_tokens[req_index] = num_prompt_tokens
218	        self.token_ids_cpu[
219	            req_index, :num_prompt_tokens] = request.prompt_token_ids
220	        start_idx = num_prompt_tokens
221	        end_idx = start_idx + len(request.output_token_ids)
222	        self.token_ids_cpu[req_index,
223	                           start_idx:end_idx] = request.output_token_ids
224	        # Number of token ids in token_ids_cpu.
225	        # NOTE(woosuk): This may include spec decode tokens.
226	        self.num_tokens[req_index] = request.num_tokens
227	        # Number of tokens without spec decode tokens.
228	        self.num_tokens_no_spec[req_index] = request.num_tokens
229	
230	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[req_index] = request.num_computed_tokens
231	        self.block_table.add_row(request.block_ids, req_index)
232	
233	        sampling_params = request.sampling_params
234	        assert sampling_params is not None, "pooling requests not supported yet"
235	        if sampling_params.sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
236	            # Avoid later division by zero.
237	            self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = -1.0
238	            self.greedy_reqs.add(req_id)
239	        else:
240	            self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.temperature
241	            self.random_reqs.add(req_id)
242	
243	        self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
244	        if sampling_params.top_p < 1:
245	            self.top_p_reqs.add(req_id)
246	        top_k = sampling_params.top_k
247	        if 0 < top_k < self.vocab_size:
248	            self.top_k_reqs.add(req_id)
249	        else:
250	            top_k = self.vocab_size
251	        self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
252	        self.min_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.min_p
253	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
254	            req_index] = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
255	        if sampling_params.min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
256	            self.min_p_reqs.add(req_id)
257	        if sampling_params.frequency_penalty != 0.0:
258	            self.frequency_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
259	        self.presence_penalties_cpu[

261	        if sampling_params.presence_penalty != 0.0:
262	            self.presence_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
263	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu[

265	        if sampling_params.repetition_penalty != 1.0:

267	        if sampling_params.min_tokens:

269	                                          sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids)

271	        # NOTE(woosuk): self.generators should not include the requests that

276	        if sampling_params.logprobs is not None:

18	class Sampler(nn.Module):
19	
20	    def __init__(self):
21	        super().__init__()
22	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
23	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
24	
25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
39	
40	        # Use float32 for the logits.
41	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
42	        # Apply allowed token ids.
43	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
44	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
45	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
46	
47	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
48	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
49	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
50	
51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
55	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
56	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
57	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
59	        sampled = sampled.long()
60	
61	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
62	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
63	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
64	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
65	
66	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
67	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)
68	
69	        # These are GPU tensors.
70	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
71	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
72	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
73	            # token per request.
74	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
75	            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
76	        )
77	        return sampler_output
78	
79	    def apply_temperature(
80	        self,
81	        logits: torch.Tensor,
82	        temp: torch.Tensor,
83	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
84	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
85	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
86	
87	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
88	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
89	
90	    def sample(
91	        self,
92	        logits: torch.Tensor,
93	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
94	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
95	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
96	
97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.

4	from typing import Optional
5	
6	import torch
7	import torch.nn as nn
8	
9	from vllm import envs
10	from vllm.logger import init_logger
11	from vllm.platforms import current_platform
12	
13	logger = init_logger(__name__)
14	
15	try:
16	    import flashinfer.sampling
17	    is_flashinfer_available = True
18	except ImportError:
19	    is_flashinfer_available = False
20	
21	
22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
119	
120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
